verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (F1-beta) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix/inner-membrane-facing subunit of the F1Fo ATP synthase. However, it is well-documented as an 'ecto-ATP synthase' on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in certain cancer cell types and other contexts (e.g., hepatocytes, endothelial cells, tumor cells), where it acts as a receptor for angiostatin and apolipoprotein A-I. This cancer/cell-state-induced ecto-display qualifies as contextual surface accessibility under cell_state_induced, even though the baseline localization is mitochondrial.
